tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, indicating possible glomerular pathology. Urine is abnormal in appearance, with high ketones and low specific gravity, raising concern for dehydration or metabolic disturbance. Hemoglobin A1c is low, possibly reflecting anemia or recent hypoglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and assessment of hydration and metabolic status.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
